extraction.one_line_summary: BAUMANN Reto won from a breakaway after surviving multiple attacks in the final 30km, including being dropped with 25km to go before working back.
tactical_patterns: Break formation took over 15km past first climb, showing teams wanted hard selection before committing riders.
Ineos controlled gap only when Baumann became virtual maglia rosa, revealing they defend jersey but won't waste energy chasing.
Multiple late-break attacks (final 30km) from FORMOLO, EVENEPOEL, MOLLEMA created accordion effect that dropped then re-admitted BAUMANN.
DUMOULIN paced final 3km after being dropped, demonstrating domestique work in break when Jumbo lacked stage wins.

rider_signals.LOPEZ Juanpe: positive: Trek-Segafredo controlled peloton through 4000m climbing without GC teammates visible, defending pink competently on terrain harder than some final week stages
rider_signals.BAUMANN Reto: positive: recovered from 25km-to-go ejection and still won sprint on 13% gradient finish, showing exceptional resilience under pressure
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: neutral: rode as super-domestique in break for Jumbo's stage hunting despite being dropped mid-race, tactical role shift confirmed
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: negative: attempted split in pink jersey group near finish but failed, suggesting Bora lacks firepower to isolate Lopez
